name: Artix Linux
tagline: Arch Linux without systemd — your init, your choice
description: Artix Linux is an Arch-based distribution that replaces systemd with a choice of alternative init systems: OpenRC, runit, s6, or dinit. For users who object to systemd on philosophical or technical grounds, Artix provides all the power of Arch — the AUR, rolling releases, pacman — with a leaner and more traditional Unix init philosophy. It supports multiple installation flavours from a minimal base to full desktop images. Artix occupies a niche but has a devoted following among those who want full control over their init infrastructure without abandoning the Arch ecosystem.
